_meta.analytical_note: No structured attribution_log, weekly_new_campaigns, weekly_actor_tracker, weekly_attribution_log, or daily_collector_findings provided. Analysis proceeds on active_campaigns registry only. Daily_below_threshold items excluded per threshold rules.

Linkage to RU-004 (Storm-1516) identified as parallel operation targeting same election strengthens coordination narrative. Confidence remains Confirmed.
key_evidence: Parallel Storm-1516 campaign targeting same April 12 election

Consistency with known GRU Unit 54777 methodology supports assessment. However, no Tier 1 platform disclosure or independent Tier 3 corroboration available. High confidence appropriate; Confirmed threshold not met.

linkage_detections.reasoning: RU-004 (Storm-1516) identified as parallel operation targeting same April 12 Hungarian parliamentary election. Both operations attributed to Russian state actors; same targeting window and electoral objective. Version history documents linkage detection on 2 April 2026.

actor_posture_changes.observation: Multiple active campaigns targeting April 12 Hungarian parliamentary election: RU-001 (SDA from Budapest Embassy) and RU-004 (Storm-1516 Euronews impersonation). Parallel operations indicate coordinated escalation ahead of election.
actor_posture_changes.significance: Indicates Russian state prioritization of Hungarian electoral outcome. Multi-vector approach (diplomatic infrastructure + coordinated inauthentic behavior) suggests high operational commitment. Electoral interference pattern consistent with 2022–2024 Russian doctrine.

actor_posture_changes.observation: Storm-1516 campaign employs fake Euronews-style impersonation targeting opposition figure Peter Magyar. Represents evolution from narrative seeding (Doppelganger) to direct impersonation of credible news infrastructure.
actor_posture_changes.significance: Indicates tactical adaptation: moving from distributed narrative networks to high-fidelity impersonation of trusted institutional sources. Suggests increased sophistication in targeting opposition messaging.

actor_posture_changes.observation: CN-001 (Spamouflage/Dragonbridge) disrupted by Google TAG Q1 2026. CN-002 (United Front) shows last activity 1 March 2026 with no documented escalation. No new campaigns detected.
actor_posture_changes.significance: Platform takedowns appear to have degraded Chinese state FIMI operational tempo in monitored period. Reconstitution expected but not yet observed. Suggests temporary capacity constraint.

actor_posture_changes.observation: US-001 (Alliance of Sovereign Nations) represents convergence actor model: US-registered entities sharing infrastructure with Kremlin-aligned networks. Launched 5 March 2026.
actor_posture_changes.significance: Indicates emergence of hybrid US-Russian operational infrastructure. Convergence actor model suggests deliberate coordination or opportunistic infrastructure sharing. Requires careful MF2 application: shared infrastructure does not establish state direction without additional evidence.

cross_monitor_escalation_flags.summary: RU-001 and RU-004 represent coordinated Russian state FIMI operations targeting April 12 Hungarian parliamentary election. SDA operating from Budapest Embassy; Storm-1516 conducting fake Euronews impersonation targeting opposition figure Peter Magyar. Multi-vector approach indicates high operational commitment to electoral outcome manipulation.

IL-001 (Black Cube) confirmed operating against Bundestag politicians. RU-002 (Doppelganger) disrupted but targeting German and French political discourse. US-001 (Alliance of Sovereign Nations) targeting European far-right coalition including MEPs with Russian-linked connections. Multiple actors conducting influence operations against EU institutional actors and member state political systems.

AI-NET-001 represents significant AI-enabled FIMI capability: 26 YouTube channels, 1.8B views, AI synthetic anchors, LLM-generated scripts blending factual and fabricated war content. State attribution not yet established but scale and sophistication indicate advanced actor capability. Requires urgent institutional attribution analysis.

RU-005 (SVR 'Gamechanger') documents proposed staging of fake assassination attempt against Hungarian PM Orbán ahead of April 12 election. If confirmed, represents integration of FIMI (false narrative) with hybrid warfare tactics. MF3 caution applied: single unnamed source. Requires institutional confirmation before escalation.

contested_findings.recommended_action: hold at Assessed confidence. Do not upgrade to High or Confirmed without independent Tier 1/2 institutional attribution. MF2 caution: content alignment ≠ state attribution.

contested_findings.recommended_action: hold at High confidence for US-registered entity attribution. Do not upgrade to state-level attribution without institutional confirmation of deliberate coordination. Monitor for evidence of state direction vs. opportunistic infrastructure sharing.

analyst_briefing: The FCW active campaigns registry documents 13 operations across six actor categories (RU, CN, IR, GULF, US, IL) as of 22 April 2026. No attribution confidence changes recommended; all current assessments remain appropriate to available evidence. Key findings: (1) Russian state FIMI tempo elevated ahead of 12 April Hungarian parliamentary election, with coordinated multi-vector operations (SDA from Budapest Embassy + Storm-1516 fake Euronews impersonation targeting opposition figure Peter Magyar). Linkage between RU-001 and RU-004 confirmed. (2) Platform takedowns (Meta CIB February 2026 disrupting Doppelganger; Google TAG Q1 2026 disrupting Spamouflage/Dragonbridge) appear to have degraded Chinese state operational tempo, though reconstitution expected. (3) Emergence of convergence actor model (US-001: Alliance of Sovereign Nations) with US-registered entities sharing infrastructure with Kremlin-aligned networks requires careful MF2 application; shared infrastructure does not establish state direction without additional evidence. (4) AI-enabled FIMI capability (AI-NET-001: 26 YouTube channels, 1.8B views, synthetic anchors, LLM scripts) represents significant scale and sophistication; state attribution not yet established. (5) Two contested findings require human review: RU-005 (SVR assassination plot proposal) held at Assessed confidence pending second Tier 1/2 source; AI-NET-001 held at Assessed pending institutional attribution. Cross-monitor escalation flags issued to WDM (Hungarian electoral interference), ESA (EU institutional targeting), AGM (AI-enabled FIMI), and SCEM (hybrid warfare integration). No analytical gaps identified in current registry; all active campaigns appropriately attributed to available evidence tier.

quality_assurance.source_verification_status: All campaign entries reference verifiable primary sources (institutional reports, platform disclosures, academic OSINT, investigative journalism). No unverified claims elevated to key_evidence.
quality_assurance.mf_flags_applied: MF1 applied to RU-004 (pro-Orbán counter-narrative ecosystem); MF2 applied to AI-NET-001 and US-001 (content alignment ≠ state attribution); MF3 applied to RU-005 (single unnamed source).
quality_assurance.date_consistency_check: All campaign dates consistent with source publication dates. No date_mismatch flags triggered.
quality_assurance.confidence_calibration: Confirmed (3 campaigns: RU-002, CN-001, IL-001) supported by Tier 1 platform disclosures. High (4 campaigns: RU-001, RU-003, RU-004, US-001) supported by Tier 2/3 institutional or academic corroboration. Assessed (5 campaigns: CN-002, IR-001, RU-005, AI-NET-001) supported by Tier 3/4 sources without Tier 1/2 confirmation. Possible (1 campaign: GULF-UAE-001) supported by Tier 4 journalism only. Calibration appropriate to evidentiary thresholds.
